title: cynoglossum nebrodense subsp. lucanum
intro: Cynoglossum nebrodense subsp. lucanum, a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae), is also known as the "Alpine Hound's Tongue" due to its distinctive tongue-like shape and its ability to grow in high-altitude mountainous regions. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been reported to help alleviate respiratory issues and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Cynoglossum nebrodense subsp. lucanum include its potential to soothe bronchitis, coughs, and colds, as well as its ability to ease menstrual cramps and improve digestion. Cynoglossum nebrodense subsp. lucanum can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
